Name: curvature_aware_mixed_precision_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Mixed Precision Routing: A Training-Free Framework for Deterministic Optimization
Short Hypothesis: By leveraging geometric subspace diagnostics and spectral quantization theory, we can develop a curvature-aware mixed precision routing framework that deterministically optimizes precision tiers without training, ensuring numerical stability and computational efficiency.
Related Work: Existing mixed precision methods often rely on heuristic approaches and training-based optimization, which can be computationally expensive and lack theoretical guarantees. Our proposal distinguishes itself by introducing a training-free framework that integrates geometric subspace diagnostics and spectral quantization theory, providing formal stability radii and PAC-Bayes generalization bounds.
Abstract: Mixed precision optimization has shown promise in reducing computational overhead while maintaining model accuracy. However, existing methods often rely on heuristic approaches and training-based optimization, which can be computationally expensive and lack theoretical guarantees. We propose a curvature-aware mixed precision routing framework that leverages geometric subspace diagnostics and spectral quantization theory to deterministically optimize precision tiers without training. This framework ensures numerical stability and computational efficiency by providing formal stability radii and PAC-Bayes generalization bounds. Our approach integrates spectral quantization theory with low-overhead implementation, bridging foundational optimization theory with deployment constraints. We validate our framework through rigorous evaluation cycles, demonstrating its effectiveness in reducing computational overhead while maintaining model accuracy.
Experiments: Experiment 1: Evaluate the framework on standard benchmarks (e.g., CIFAR-10, ImageNet) to measure computational overhead and model accuracy. Metrics: FLOPs, accuracy, and variance across multiple seeds.

Experiment 2: Perform spectral diagnostics to quantify gradient covariance decay, Hessian conditioning, and feature entanglement. Metrics: Condition number, gradient covariance decay rate, and feature entanglement score.

Experiment 3: Compare the framework against existing mixed precision methods to demonstrate improvements in computational efficiency and numerical stability. Metrics: FLOPs, accuracy, and numerical stability metrics.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risk of the framework not generalizing across different architectures and datasets.
Limitation in scalability to larger models due to the single-GPU validation constraint.
Potential overhead from spectral diagnostics impacting computational efficiency.
